title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured

input_row.title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured
input_row.description: Where etcd encryption is used, appropriate providers should be configured.
input_row.rationale: Where etcd encryption is used, it is important to ensure that the appropriate set of encryption providers is used. Currently, the aescbc, kms and secretbox are likely to be appropriate options. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Get the EncryptionConfig file set for --encryption-provider-config argument. Verify that aescbc, kms or secretbox is set as the encryption provider for all the desired resources.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and configure a EncryptionConfig file. In this file, choose aescbc, kms or secretbox as the encryption provider. Default Value: By default, no encryption provider is set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/ 2. https://acotten.com/post/kube17-security 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/features/issues/92 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/#providers
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_encryption_provider_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver process arguments to identify the value of the `--encryption-provider-config` flag. This can be done by:
  - Inspecting the kube-apiserver Pod spec in the kube-system namespace (if running as a static pod or deployment), e.g.:
    ```
    kubectl -n kube-system get pods -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
    ```
    or
    ```
    kubectl -n kube-system get pod <kube-apiserver-pod> -o yaml
    ```
    and look for the `--encryption-provider-config` argument.
  - If not running as a pod, this may require access to the node to inspect the process, but in most managed or modern clusters, kube-apiserver runs as a pod.

- Retrieve the referenced EncryptionConfig file (the path specified in `--encryption-provider-config`). If the file is mounted as a ConfigMap or Secret, it can be retrieved via:
  ```
  kubectl -n kube-system get configmap <name> -o yaml
  ```
  or
  ```
  kubectl -n kube-system get secret <name> -o yaml
  ```
  If the file is not accessible via Kubernetes resources, this step may require node access (see note below).

- Parse the EncryptionConfig YAML to verify that for all resources listed under `resources:`, the `providers:` array includes at least one of the following as the first provider:
  - `aescbc`
  - `kms`
  - `secretbox`
  (The first provider is used for new writes.)

- Pass Criteria:
  - The `--encryption-provider-config` flag is set on all kube-apiserver instances.
  - The referenced EncryptionConfig file is present and valid.
  - For all resources in the EncryptionConfig, the first provider is `aescbc`, `kms`, or `secretbox`.

- Fail Criteria:
  - The `--encryption-provider-config` flag is missing or not set.
  - The EncryptionConfig file is missing, invalid, or not referenced.
  - The first provider for any resource is not `aescbc`, `kms`, or `secretbox` (e.g., `identity` or `aesgcm` as first provider).

**Note:** If the EncryptionConfig file is not exposed via Kubernetes resources (ConfigMap/Secret/Pod volume), node-level access may be required, which would make this a Manual check. However, in most enterprise and managed Kubernetes environments, the configuration is accessible via pod specs and mounted resources, making this check Automated.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
